title: Fix for overwriting LD_PRELOAD if it contains libscalene.so and other values
body: It looks like in some cases setup_preload gets called more than once, so it was adding `libscalene.so` to `LD_PRELOAD` the first time then later replacing `LD_PRELOAD`  with just `libscalene.so`, this fixes that case.

diff_between_base_and_last.patch:
@@ -68,11 +68,10 @@ def get_preload_environ(args: argparse.Namespace) -> Dict[str, str]:
                     env['LD_LIBRARY_PATH'] = f'{library_path}:{os.environ["LD_LIBRARY_PATH"]}'
 
                 new_ld_preload = 'libscalene.so'
-                if "LD_PRELOAD" in os.environ and new_ld_preload not in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]:
-                    old_ld_preload = os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]
-                    env["LD_PRELOAD"] = new_ld_preload + ":" + old_ld_preload
-                else:
+                if "LD_PRELOAD" not in os.environ:
                     env["LD_PRELOAD"] = new_ld_preload
+                elif new_ld_preload not in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"].split(":"):
+                    env["LD_PRELOAD"] = f'{new_ld_preload}:{os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]}'
                 # Disable command-line specified PYTHONMALLOC.
                 if "PYTHONMALLOC" in os.environ:
                     # Since the environment dict is updated

commits.message: Fix for overwriting LD_PRELOAD if it contains libscalene.so and other values

files.patch:
@@ -71,6 +71,8 @@ def get_preload_environ(args: argparse.Namespace) -> Dict[str, str]:
                 if "LD_PRELOAD" in os.environ and new_ld_preload not in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]:
                     old_ld_preload = os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]
                     env["LD_PRELOAD"] = new_ld_preload + ":" + old_ld_preload
+                elif "LD_PRELOAD" in os.environ and new_ld_preload in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]:
+                    env["LD_PRELOAD"] = os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]
                 else:
                     env["LD_PRELOAD"] = new_ld_preload
                 # Disable command-line specified PYTHONMALLOC.

commits.message:
Improve logic for setting LD_PRELOAD

- split on : as per https://github.com/plasma-umass/scalene/pull/925#discussion_r2136075173
- simplify conditions

files.patch:
@@ -68,13 +68,10 @@ def get_preload_environ(args: argparse.Namespace) -> Dict[str, str]:
                     env['LD_LIBRARY_PATH'] = f'{library_path}:{os.environ["LD_LIBRARY_PATH"]}'
 
                 new_ld_preload = 'libscalene.so'
-                if "LD_PRELOAD" in os.environ and new_ld_preload not in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]:
-                    old_ld_preload = os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]
-                    env["LD_PRELOAD"] = new_ld_preload + ":" + old_ld_preload
-                elif "LD_PRELOAD" in os.environ and new_ld_preload in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]:
-                    env["LD_PRELOAD"] = os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]
-                else:
+                if "LD_PRELOAD" not in os.environ:
                     env["LD_PRELOAD"] = new_ld_preload
+                elif new_ld_preload not in os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"].split(":"):
+                    env["LD_PRELOAD"] = f'{new_ld_preload}:{os.environ["LD_PRELOAD"]}'
                 # Disable command-line specified PYTHONMALLOC.
                 if "PYTHONMALLOC" in os.environ:
                     # Since the environment dict is updated
